macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Micron Technology (MU) Drops 12% Ahead of Crucial Q3 Earnings Report
items.summary: Memory chipmaker Micron Technology fell as much as 12% on Tuesday despite being up over 270% for the year, as investors await its Q3 earnings report on Wednesday, June 24, which is seen as a critical test for AI market dynamics[1][2].

items.title: KOSPI Volatility Hits 90-Handle as Chip Trade Uncertainty Mounts
items.summary: South Korea's KOSPI index experienced extreme volatility with a 90-handle reading, implying a daily 5.5% move, driven by uncertainty in the chip trade and a 10% drop from the previous day, the worst since early March[2].

items.title: US President Trump Promotes 'Best Economy Ever' Agenda in Pennsylvania Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: President Trump is promoting his economic agenda in Pennsylvania, citing Labor Department data showing better-than-expected job growth, while dismissing inflation concerns fueled by the Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure[3].

items.title: Philippines Central Bank Hikes Rates Twice This Year to Combat 7.2% Inflation
items.summary: The Philippines' central bank opted to hike rates twice in 2026 to address inflation that peaked at 7.2% in April, despite GDP growth falling to a low 2.8% in Q1, the lowest since 2009 excluding the pandemic[4].

items.title: China's Retail Sales Fall 0.6% in May as Trade War Shifts Export Reliance to EU
items.summary: China's retail sales declined 0.6% year-on-year in May, the first drop since December 2022, while exports surged 19.4% as the US-China trade war has made Chinese exporters more reliant on the EU as a 'consumer of last resort'[5].

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs Hit Record Outflows Amid Worst Month Since Launch
items.summary: US Bitcoin ETFs experienced record outflows over the past 30 days, marking the worst month since their launch two years ago, with prices consolidating near lows around $62,500 after breaking below the $63,000 ascending support line.

items.title: Bitcoin Death Cross and Bearish Patterns Signal Potential Drop to $45,000
items.summary: Bitcoin is forming a major death cross between the 50 and 200 daily moving averages while breaking a bear flag pattern on the 4-hour chart, with analysts warning of a possible drop to $45,000—the 2022 bear market support level.

items.title: Congress Schedules Clarity Act Hearing for July 17; White House Races to Pass Bipartisan Crypto Bill
items.summary: Congress has scheduled a hearing on the Clarity Act for July 17, 2026, while the White House, led by crypto adviser Patrick Wit, is negotiating with Democrats to pass the bill before midterms, though an ethics rule restricting crypto profits for elected officials remains a sticking point.

items.title: Ethereum and XRP Show Continued Weakness with Support Tests at $1.5K and $68
items.summary: Ethereum is testing critical support between $1.5K–$1.6K with RSI nearing oversold territory, while XRP breaks below its ascending support line, confirming a longer-term bearish trend with key support at $68 and next levels at $61–$63.

items.title: Chainlink Flips Cardano by Market Cap Amid Market Rebalancing
items.summary: Chainlink has surpassed Cardano in market capitalization as the crypto market rebalances, with Bitcoin holding the 200-week moving average and remaining above February lows despite short-term pullbacks.

items.title: Genius Act Passed, Providing Regulatory Clarity for Stablecoins
items.summary: The Genius Act has been passed, delivering regulatory clarity for stablecoins and contributing to renewed market excitement around stablecoin yields, though its impact on broader crypto adoption remains under evaluation.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Releases NotebookLM 2.0: A Fully Agentic AI Researcher Powered by Gemini 3.5
items.summary: Google has transformed NotebookLM into a powerful AI research agent that automatically finds sources, executes code, analyzes data, generates presentations, and creates cinematic AI videos using the upgraded Gemini 3.5 model.

items.title: Meta Hires Four OpenAI Researchers in Major Talent Acquisition
items.summary: Meta has hired four former OpenAI researchers—Shengjia Zhao, Jiahui Yu, Shuchao Bi, and Hongyu Ren—marking a significant shift in AI talent between the two companies.

items.title: OpenAI's ChatGPT Experiences Global Downtime Amid User Loading Issues
items.summary: Users worldwide reported issues loading prompts on OpenAI's ChatGPT, causing service disruptions and highlighting potential infrastructure challenges for the platform.

items.title: Anthropic Plans ID Verification for Select Users Following Claude Mythos Delay
items.summary: Anthropic will begin requesting ID verification from some users, while the company previously announced it won't release its new Claude Mythos AI model to the public due to safety concerns.

items.title: Siemens CEO Highlights Industrial AI's Expansion to Factory Floors Globally
items.summary: Siemens CEO Roland Busch emphasized that AI is moving beyond chatbots to factory floors, boosting productivity in sectors like semiconductors, pharmaceuticals, aerospace, and automotive industries through digital twins and robotics.

items.title: US-UAE Multi-Billion Dollar AI Data Campus Deal Remains Unfinalized
items.summary: Sources indicate that the proposed multi-billion dollar AI data campus deal between the US and UAE is far from finalized, reflecting ongoing complexities in international AI infrastructure investments.

items.title: Apple to Open Underlying AI Technology to Developers
items.summary: Apple announced plans to open up its underlying AI technology to developers, potentially enabling broader integration and innovation across its ecosystem.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: New VR Games Announced for Quest 3, PSVR 2, and PCVR Including 'Breachers Outbreak' and 'Dagger Woods VR'
items.summary: A lineup of upcoming VR titles was revealed today, featuring intense shooters, PvPvE extraction games, and co-op survival adventures like 'Breachers Outbreak' and 'Dagger Woods VR' across Meta Quest, PSVR 2, and PCVR platforms.

items.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Officially Announced with $50 Price Increase Amid Market Shifts
items.summary: Nintendo reacted to recent market changes, including Sony's PS5 price hikes and Steam Machine's $1,000+ launch price, by officially announcing a $50 price increase for the Nintendo Switch 2.

items.title: Steam Machine Launches with Over $1,000 Starting Price, Significantly Higher Than Valve's Original Target
items.summary: Valve unveiled the official price for the Steam Machine yesterday, revealing a starting point north of $1,000, which is significantly more than the company originally intended to charge.

items.title: Retro Gaming Handhelds Evolve with Retroid Pocket Nova Design Reveal and Anbernic Snapdragon G1 Gen 2 Launch
items.summary: The retro gaming sector saw major updates including the Retroid Pocket Nova's new design and display specs, and Anbernic's new Snapdragon G1 Gen 2 handheld for improved Android and emulation performance.

items.title: Prime Day Gaming Deals Now Available with Extensive Opportunities to Save on Games and Hardware
items.summary: Prime Day has officially begun, offering a vast array of gaming deals and discounts that IGN's daily fix has curated for gamers looking to save on titles and equipment.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Confirms Deal with Intel to Build Chips Amid US Manufacturing Push
items.summary: President Trump confirmed that Apple has agreed to manufacture chips with Intel, marking a significant shift in US semiconductor strategy to bring production back from Taiwan.

items.title: Amazon Prime Day Launches with Major Deals on Routers, Tablets, and Apple Products
items.summary: Amazon Prime Day has begun, featuring discounts over 35% on Samsung's Galaxy Tab S10 FE+, top routers from TP-Link, and Apple products hitting all-time lows including AirPods and iPad Air.

items.title: Apple's New CEO John Ternus Pushes for 'Truly Incredible' Device Designs
items.summary: Incoming Apple CEO John Ternus, who previously oversaw the design team, is pushing for revolutionary new iPhone and device designs, potentially shaking up the company's product lineup.

items.title: Nvidia Solves Data Center Water Problem in Major Tech Breakthrough
items.summary: Nvidia announced it has solved the critical water usage problem in data centers, a major development for data center infrastructure technology.

items.title: SpaceX Trading Frenzy Boosts Elon Musk's Wealth by Billions in One Day
items.summary: SpaceX's trading frenzy generated more wealth for Elon Musk in a single day than Bill Gates' entire net worth, though skepticism remains about the company's valuation.

items.title: Samsung Stock Surges on Share Buyback Plan Reports
items.summary: Samsung shares rose significantly following local media reports about a major share buyback plan, making it the top performer in the Hang Seng Tech index.
